crime: The harvest-queen learned what her claiming could bring, and made her a procurer — every taking put on show at the window corner as the harvest-queen's own. One midday the harvest-queen ordered the next before the whole circle — the tall girl's embroidered handkerchief, by Friday — and turned away without waiting for a reply.
The harvest-queen kept a jar of sweet syrup for tea. No hand but the harvest-queen's own ever moved it.

One evening she claimed the heart-tincture from the medical room. Its keeper carried the vial out, placed it before her, and walked away half-certain the trip had been the keeper's own idea. A spoonful steadies a weak heart; a whole vial stops a healthy one.

Next morning she claimed the jar. The harvest-queen carried it to her door and set it down; she took it behind her door, poured the whole vial over the thick syrup — it sat as an unmixed layer at the top — and set the jar back out. The harvest-queen, still muddled — saying she had meant to show it to someone — picked it up and carried it home; it was the only time the jar had ever left its owner's keeping.

At midday the harvest-queen spooned from the top of the jar, stirred it into tea, and drank before the circle. The harvest-queen was dead among the collection within the hour — one spoonful off the surface had drawn up nearly all the tincture.
The keeper's shelf stood one vial short, and the keeper could not settle whether she had lent it. The syrup smelled faintly of the tincture — the keeper knew her own bitters.
The vial had left a shut room no girl came asking at. The jar had left its owner's keeping once in its life, that very morning — and the door where its owner stood muddled was the witch's own.
fields.epitaph: She put a price on everything — except the taking itself.
fields.epithet: The Girl No One Could Afford
execution: A ring of empty iron seats circled the execution stand. Above them, a single hand on a track.
The hand closed on her, carried her to the first seat, and set her down before it. It took a new grip and carried her to the next.
Seat after seat the hand moved her, and at each one the grip tightened.
The hand that held her belonged to no one.
At the last seat the grip closed and did not open. It tightened until she was crushed.

magic.name: Requisition
magic.text: When she claims a thing belonging to another, its owner carries it to her, sets it down, and leaves half-certain the errand was their own.
